info.title: Private Payments API
info.description:
REST API for building private SPL token transactions on Solana and MagicBlock ephemeral rollups.

Documentation: https://docs.magicblock.gg/pages/private-ephemeral-rollups-pers/how-to-guide/quickstart

cluster.description: Optional. Use `mainnet` for BASE_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_RPC_URL, `devnet` for BASE_DEVNET_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_DEVNET_RPC_URL, or provide a custom http(s) RPC URL to override the base RPC while keeping the configured ephemeral RPC.

mint.description: Optional. Defaults to Solana USDC on mainnet: EPjFWdd5AufqSSqeM2qN1xzybapC8G4wEGGkZwyTDt1v. On devnet it defaults to devnet USDC: 4zMMC9srt5Ri5X14GAgXhaHii3GnPAEERYPJgZJDncDU.

amount.description: Base-unit amount as an integer JSON value with minimum 1.

validator.description: Optional. Defaults to the selected ephemeral RPC identity resolved via `getIdentity`.

cluster.description: Optional. Use `mainnet` for BASE_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_RPC_URL, `devnet` for BASE_DEVNET_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_DEVNET_RPC_URL, or provide a custom http(s) RPC URL to override the base RPC while keeping the configured ephemeral RPC.

amount.description: Base-unit amount as an integer JSON value with minimum 1.

validator.description: Optional. Defaults to the selected ephemeral RPC identity resolved via `getIdentity`.

cluster.description: Optional. Use `mainnet` for BASE_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_RPC_URL, `devnet` for BASE_DEVNET_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_DEVNET_RPC_URL, or provide a custom http(s) RPC URL to override the base RPC while keeping the configured ephemeral RPC.

validator.description: Optional. Defaults to the selected ephemeral RPC identity resolved via `getIdentity`.

cluster.description: Optional. Use `mainnet` for BASE_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_RPC_URL, `devnet` for BASE_DEVNET_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_DEVNET_RPC_URL, or provide a custom http(s) RPC URL to override the base RPC while keeping the configured ephemeral RPC.

amount.description: Base-unit amount as an integer JSON value with minimum 1.

validator.description: Optional. When this transfer route needs a validator and none is provided, the API resolves it from the selected ephemeral RPC via `getIdentity`.

memo.description: Optional. Appends a final Memo Program instruction with this UTF-8 message.

minDelayMs.description: Optional. Private transfer only. Defaults to 0.

maxDelayMs.description: Optional. Private transfer only. Defaults to 0 when omitted, or to minDelayMs when minDelayMs is set.

clientRefId.description: Optional. Private transfer only. Encrypted client reference ID that can be used to confirm a payment.

split.description: Optional. Private transfer only. Defaults to 1. Must be between 1 and 15.

gasless.description: Optional. When true, the API uses the configured sponsor as transaction fee payer and prepends a relay-fee token transfer to the sponsor ATA.

legacy.description: Optional. Defaults to false. When true, skips lookup-table compilation and returns a legacy transaction.

visibility.description: `public` (default) proxies Jupiter/Metis as-is. `private` routes the output through a scheduled private transfer.

content.text: Built an unsigned SPL deposit transaction.

get.description: Generate a challenge string for the wallet to sign.

parameters.name: pubkey
parameters.description: The public key of the wallet that will read private data.

post.description: Login the wallet to the Private Ephemeral Rollup.
Deposit SPL tokens from Solana into an ephemeral rollup.
Withdraw SPL tokens from an ephemeral rollup back to Solana.
Build an unsigned base-chain transaction that initializes and delegates a validator-scoped transfer queue for a mint.
Transfer SPL tokens publicly or privately through an ephemeral rollup. Pass an optional `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for transfers that need to connect to the Private Ephemeral Rollup.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Optional. Authentication token for requests that need to connect to the Private Ephemeral Rollup.

400.description: Build error or unsupported transfer route (`UNSUPPORTED_TRANSFER_ROUTE`)

get.description: Get the balance for the owner's ATA on the base RPC.

parameters.name: cluster
parameters.description: Optional. Use `mainnet` for BASE_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_RPC_URL, `devnet` for BASE_DEVNET_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_DEVNET_RPC_URL, or provide a custom http(s) RPC URL to override the base RPC while keeping the configured ephemeral RPC.

get.description: Get the balance for the owner's ATA on the ephemeral RPC. Requires authentication via the Private Ephemeral Rollup login flow.

parameters.name: cluster
parameters.description: Optional. Use `mainnet` for BASE_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_RPC_URL, `devnet` for BASE_DEVNET_RPC_URL and EPHEMERAL_DEVNET_RPC_URL, or provide a custom http(s) RPC URL to override the base RPC while keeping the configured ephemeral RPC.

400.description: Missing auth token (`MISSING_AUTH_TOKEN`) or other query error

get.description: Check whether the validator-scoped transfer queue exists for a mint on the ephemeral RPC.

parameters.name: validator
parameters.description: Optional. Defaults to the selected ephemeral RPC identity resolved via `getIdentity`.

get.description: Get a swap quote between two SPL mints. Proxies the configured Triton Metis Swap API.

post.description: Build an unsigned swap transaction from a quote. Supports `visibility="public"` (default upstream pass-through) and `visibility="private"` (forces output into a program-owned stash ATA and appends a `schedule_private_transfer` instruction).

400.description: Invalid request (missing or conflicting private-transfer fields)

post.description:
Stateless Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint.

Implementation details:
- Each `POST /mcp` request creates a fresh `McpServer`, handles the JSON-RPC exchange, then closes it.
- The transport runs with `sessionIdGenerator: undefined` and `enableJsonResponse: true`, so this server does not issue or require `mcp-session-id` headers.
- `GET /mcp` returns the info document and `GET /.well-known/mcp.json` returns the discovery document.
- Clients can use `Accept: application/json` with `Content-Type: application/json`.

Typical flow:
1. `initialize`
2. `notifications/initialized`
3. `tools/list` or `tools/call`

content.text: Built an unsigned SPL deposit transaction.

202.description: Notification accepted; no response body is returned.

400.description: Invalid JSON or invalid JSON-RPC request
